message:
style(mvcc,btrfs,journal): rustfmt formatting + close beads issues bd-1054 and bd-25zj

Applies rustfmt formatting across several crates:
- ffs-btrfs: reformatted match arm destructuring for BtrfsExtentData::Inline
- ffs-journal: whitespace cleanup and line wrapping for incomplete_transactions stat
- ffs-mvcc compression: reformatted resolve_data_with signature and test assertions
- ffs-mvcc lib: reformatted read_visible signature and test assertions to multi-line
- ffs-mvcc sharded: line-wrapped resolve_data_with call chain
- ffs-mvcc tests: line-wrapped read_visible call chain in flush_epoch_guard test

Beads: closes bd-1054 (checked arithmetic for ffs-alloc casts) and
bd-25zj (hardened extent header tail_off overflow).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-10t","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement inode read (ffs-inode) from disk image","description":"Goal: read an ext4 inode by inode number and return a typed struct with enough info for getattr + extent mapping.

 {"id":"bd-1c4y.4","title":"Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy","description":"# Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the S3-FIFO epic (bd-1c4y). Currently has E2E test (bd-1c4y.3) and benchmark (bd-1c4y.2) but NO unit tests for the three-queue eviction logic, scan resistance, or promotion/demotion paths.

## TEST PLAN

### Queue Structure Tests
1. New entry goes to small queue (S)
2. Second access promotes from S to main queue (M)
3. Eviction from S goes to ghost queue (G)
4. Re-access from G promotes to M (scan resistance)
5. Queue size ratios maintained (S=10%, M=90%)

### Eviction Tests
6. Eviction selects from S first (FIFO order)
7. S empty: eviction falls through to M (FIFO order)
8. Ghost queue bounded (does not grow unbounded)
9. Cache at capacity: insert triggers eviction

### Scan Resistance Tests
10. Sequential scan does not pollute M queue
11. Hot entries survive sequential scan
12. Mixed workload: hot set retained, scan set evicted

### Integration with ARC
13. S3-FIFO can replace ARC eviction policy
14. Cache hit/miss metrics correct under S3-FIFO

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 14 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log queue sizes and contents

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 14 tests pass
2. Scan resistance verified (hot entries retained)
3. No unbounded memory growth","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberShore","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:17.235905478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T19:51:25.639424152Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T19:51:25.639405978Z","close_reason":"Completed: S3-FIFO unit tests + clippy/fmt gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","s3fifo","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:17.235905478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.663798987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cc","title":"FUSE: Define MVCC snapshot/transaction policy for FUSE operations","description":"Goal: decide how MVCC integrates with the kernel request model.

Questions:
- Does each FUSE op run at a single snapshot (read-only), or do we start a transaction per open()/release()?
- How do we handle readdir+lookup consistency?

Deliverables:
- A documented policy in COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC.
- Implementation hooks in ffs-fuse to acquire snapshot/tx per request.

Acceptance:
- Concurrency tests show consistent reads under concurrent writers (once writes exist).
- Policy is simple enough to reason about and test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:39.467984753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:50:37.112290445Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:50:37.112268945Z","close_reason":"done: documented Phase 7A policy + implemented FsOps request-scope hooks in ffs-fuse with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cc","depends_on_id":"bd-2t1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:52.176803421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1cq","title":"Track: btrfs On-Disk Parsing + Mapping (ffs-ondisk)","description":"Implement btrfs superblock parsing, sys_chunk_array bootstrap mapping, single-device logical->physical mapping, and basic B-tree node/item parsing for read-only discovery.\n\nAcceptance: can open a btrfs image, validate superblock, map a logical address via sys_chunk, and iterate root tree items without panics; all guarded by fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:41.112572499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:27:28.404663163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:27:28.404641172Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-1fo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.414637386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.259271763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-kdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.337640505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-o76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:03.495838876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1cq","depends_on_id":"bd-z7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:19:16.963240254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-1cq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"btrfs bootstrap is different from ext4: you must parse sys_chunk_array to map logical->physical before you can even read the trees.

Early wins:
- parse superblock + sys_chunk
- map root/chunk_root
- parse nodes and walk root tree (read-only)

Multi-device and RAID profiles remain explicitly excluded until read-only parity is solid.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:38Z"}]}

Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ds","title":"ffs-types: Finalize InodeNumber strategy (ext4 u32 vs btrfs u64)","description":"Context: ext4 stores inode numbers as u32; btrfs uses u64 objectids. The code currently standardizes on InodeNumber(u64) in ffs-types.

Goal: make the representation choice explicit and safe:
- keep InodeNumber(u64) canonical, but add per-format wrappers (Ext4InodeNumber(u32), BtrfsObjectId(u64)) OR
- switch to an enum/typed wrapper that prevents mixing domains.

Deliverables:
- Chosen representation documented in ffs-types docs + COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC.
- Conversion functions at parsing boundaries.
- Tests proving no silent truncation or sign/width bugs.

Acceptance:
- No crate invents its own inode/objectid types.
- Public APIs do not require callers to guess whether a u64 is ext4 or btrfs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:31.298330901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:54.151204622Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:00:54.151178934Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

 {"id":"bd-1mdk.4","title":"E2E benchmark: Bw-Tree vs locked B-tree extent index throughput","description":"# E2E benchmark: Bw-Tree vs locked B-tree extent index throughput

## GOAL
Prove that the Bw-Tree extent index provides superior concurrent throughput compared to the current locked B-tree implementation. This is the justification benchmark for the entire Bw-Tree epic.

## BENCHMARK SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Read-Heavy (95% lookup, 5% insert)
- Simulate metadata-heavy workload (readdir, stat)
- Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
- Measure: lookups/sec, p50/p95/p99 lookup latency
- Expected: Bw-Tree scales linearly (readers never block); locked B-tree saturates

### Scenario 2: Write-Heavy (30% lookup, 70% insert/delete)
- Simulate file creation/deletion workload
- Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
- Measure: operations/sec, CAS retry rate, consolidation frequency
- Expected: Bw-Tree outperforms at >= 4 threads (CAS contention < lock contention)

### Scenario 3: Mixed Workload (realistic filesystem)
- 50% lookup, 30% insert, 10% delete, 10% range scan
- Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
- Measure: aggregate ops/sec, per-operation latency breakdown
- Expected: Bw-Tree advantage increases with thread count

### Scenario 4: Single-Threaded Baseline
- Pure sequential operations (no concurrency)
- Measure: Bw-Tree overhead vs B-tree (delta chain traversal cost)
- Expected: B-tree faster single-threaded (simpler structure); Bw-Tree within 2x

### Scenario 5: Consolidation Overhead
- Measure: consolidation frequency, duration, and impact on concurrent operations
- Stress test: maximum write rate with consolidation enabled/disabled
- Expected: consolidation adds < 10% overhead at steady state

## METRICS
- Operations per second (throughput)
- Latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) per operation type
- CAS retry rate (contention measure)
- Consolidation frequency and duration
- Memory usage (peak RSS)
- Scaling efficiency: ops/sec(N threads) / ops/sec(1 thread)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Bw-Tree outperforms locked B-tree at >= 4 concurrent threads for read-heavy workload
- [ ] Bw-Tree single-threaded overhead is within 2x of locked B-tree
- [ ] CAS retry rate < 10% under realistic workloads
- [ ] Consolidation overhead < 10% at steady state
- [ ] Results stored as JSON CI artifacts for regression tracking
- [ ] Benchmark harness is deterministic and reproducible
- [ ] Logging: tracing output captures per-scenario timing and contention metrics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:51.899776011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:19:49.752844954Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:19:49.752760887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","bwtree","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:51.899776011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdk.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:51.899776011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-1mdk.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: quality_assurance
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.1 Optimization Loop + 0.7 Artifact Contract + 6.12 Proptest
EV score: 50 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: bench_runs>=10, sample_floor=30 for noisy paths
Fallback trigger: golden-only gate, disable adaptive controller deployment

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-23s","title":"Epic: ext4 Advanced Semantics (htree, symlink, xattr, permissions)","description":"Follow-on work to reach stronger ext4 parity beyond the MVP.

Scope examples:
- htree indexed dirs
- symlinks
- xattrs

 {"id":"bd-38k","title":"Implement SSI write-skew detection with rw-antidependency tracking","description":"# Implement SSI write-skew detection with rw-antidependency tracking

## GOAL
Implement Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI) to detect write-skew anomalies that FCW alone cannot catch.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
FCW detects write-write conflicts. But write-skew is possible:

```
T1: reads A, writes B
T2: reads B, writes A
Both commit under SI -> non-serializable!
```

SSI (Cahill-Rohm-Fekete 2008) detects this via rw-antidependency cycles.

## FORMAL MODEL

### Definitions
- **rw-antidependency T1 -rw-> T2**: T1 read block B at version V, T2 later wrote V for B
- **Dangerous structure**: Consecutive rw-antideps forming a cycle

### Detection Algorithm
At commit time for transaction T:
1. For each block B in T.write_set:
   - Find all transactions T that read B at earlier version
   - If any such T is concurrent and has rw-dep on T -> dangerous
2. For each block B in T.read_set:
   - If committed transaction T wrote B after T read it -> T has rw-dep
3. If T participates in a dangerous structure -> abort

### Implementation
```rust
pub struct SsiTracker {
    /// Active transactions and their read/write sets
    active: BTreeMap<TxnId, TxnSets>,
    /// Recent commits for rw-dep detection
    recent_commits: VecDeque<CommittedTxn>,
}

impl SsiTracker {
    pub fn check_commit(&self, txn: &Transaction) -> Result<(), SsiConflict>;
    pub fn record_commit(&mut self, txn_id: TxnId, write_set: &BTreeSet<BlockNumber>);
    pub fn prune_old(&mut self, watermark: CommitSeq);
}
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Write-skew detected and aborted
2. Unit: Non-conflicting concurrent txns commit
3. Integration: Classic write-skew scenario fails
4. Lab: Deterministic interleaving tests

## LOGGING
- SSI conflict (warn): txn_ids involved, blocks
- Dangerous structure (debug): cycle description

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Write-skew anomalies impossible
2. False positive rate < 1% for non-conflicting workloads
3. Overhead < 5% vs FCW-only
4. Deterministic under Lab runtime","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:52:29.715984687Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:25:59.999909898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:25:59.999832684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mvcc","ssi"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38k","depends_on_id":"bd-22w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.619245746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-38k","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:38.787722569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-38k","depends_on_id":"bd-kro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:26.070401588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-38o","title":"Implement FUSE request batching and readahead","description":"# Implement FUSE request batching and readahead

## GOAL
Optimize FUSE performance through request batching and predictive readahead.

## BACKGROUND
FUSE overhead is ~5-10μs per round-trip. Mitigation strategies:
1. Batch multiple reads into single I/O
2. Predictive readahead for sequential access
3. Attribute caching with TTL

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Sequential Access Detection
```rust
pub struct AccessPredictor {
    history: LruCache<InodeNumber, AccessPattern>,
}

pub struct AccessPattern {
    last_offset: u64,
    last_size: u64,
    sequential_count: u32,
    direction: Direction, // Forward or Backward
}

impl AccessPredictor {
    pub fn record(&mut self, inode: InodeNumber, offset: u64, size: u64);
    pub fn predict_next(&self, inode: InodeNumber) -> Option<ReadaheadHint>;
}
```

### 2. Readahead Implementation
```rust
pub struct ReadaheadManager {
    pending: BTreeMap<(InodeNumber, BlockNumber), ReadaheadState>,
    max_ahead: usize,  // Default: 16 blocks
}

impl ReadaheadManager {
    pub fn trigger(&mut self, hint: ReadaheadHint, cache: &ArcCache, cx: &Cx);
    pub fn complete(&mut self, inode: InodeNumber, block: BlockNumber);
    pub fn cancel(&mut self, inode: InodeNumber);
}
```

### 3. Request Coalescing
- Merge adjacent read requests into single I/O
- Merge adjacent write requests before flush
- Configurable coalesce window

### 4. Attribute Caching
```rust
pub struct AttrCache {
    cache: LruCache<InodeNumber, (FileAttr, Instant)>,
    ttl: Duration,  // Default: 1 second
}
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Sequential detection accuracy
2. Integration: Readahead improves sequential throughput
3. Integration: Coalescing reduces I/O count
4. Benchmark: Before/after comparison

## LOGGING
- Sequential detected (trace): inode, confidence
- Readahead triggered (trace): inode, blocks
- Coalesce (trace): request count, merged size

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Sequential read throughput >2x vs no readahead
2. Readahead does not hurt random workloads
3. Memory bounded (max pending readahead)
4. TTL expires correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:56.985822386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T14:50:46.608125172Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T14:50:46.608101308Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","optimization","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38o","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:55:47.151058902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-38o","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:38.363088278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-38o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:30Z"}]}
